rs4244285.recommendation_het: Intermediate metabolizer — consider alternative to clopidogrel; standard PPI dosing likely adequate.
rs4244285.recommendation_hom: Poor metabolizer — clopidogrel contraindicated (use prasugrel/ticagrelor); consider PPI dose reduction.

rs12248560.recommendation_het: Rapid metabolizer — enhanced clopidogrel activation; may need higher SSRI doses.
rs12248560.recommendation_hom: Ultra-rapid metabolizer — increased clopidogrel effect; reduced voriconazole efficacy.

rs1065852.recommendation_het: Intermediate metabolizer — reduced codeine→morphine conversion; standard tamoxifen may suffice.
rs1065852.recommendation_hom: Poor metabolizer — codeine/tramadol ineffective; tamoxifen activation impaired; use alternatives.

rs776746.recommendation_het: Intermediate expressor — may need standard tacrolimus dosing.
rs776746.recommendation_hom: CYP3A5 non-expressor — standard tacrolimus dosing; expressors need ~50% higher doses.

rs9923231.recommendation_het: Intermediate warfarin sensitivity — consider ~25% dose reduction from standard.
rs9923231.recommendation_hom: High warfarin sensitivity — consider ~50% dose reduction; start low, monitor INR closely.

rs4149056.recommendation_het: Increased statin myopathy risk — avoid simvastatin >20mg; consider rosuvastatin.
rs4149056.recommendation_hom: High statin myopathy risk — avoid simvastatin entirely; use low-dose rosuvastatin or pravastatin.

rs1045642.recommendation_het: Slightly altered drug bioavailability — monitor digoxin levels if prescribed.
rs1045642.recommendation_hom: Reduced P-glycoprotein function — increased bioavailability of substrate drugs; dose adjustments may be needed.

rs3745274.recommendation_het: Intermediate metabolizer — consider efavirenz dose reduction to 400mg.
rs3745274.recommendation_hom: Poor metabolizer — efavirenz 200-400mg recommended; higher methadone levels expected.
